name: Corn Chowder for 2
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Corn (2 small cobs or canned)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the corn: Make a deep cut down the center of each row of kernels on the cobs. Remove the kernels and place them in a large bowl. If using canned or frozen corn, chop or mash it before adding to the bowl.
2. In a saucepan, lightly saute the chopped onions and cubed potatoes in butter over medium heat until they are lightly browned (about 10 minutes).
3. Add paprika, kelp powder (or salt), pepper, and water to the saucepan. Stir well, then cover and simmer for 8 minutes.
4. Add the corn, milk, honey, and bay leaf to the saucepan. Gently simmer for an additional 20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
5. Remove the bay leaf before serving. This recipe serves 2 portions, each containing approximately 1 1/2 starch exchange, 1 fat, and 1/2 milk. (Source: Low-Cost Natural Foods, Rodale Press; Tested by Elizabeth Rodier in August 1993)
